summary:
CRISPR/Cas12a can detect EGFR mutations in circulating DNA due to its precise sequence-specific target recognition enabled by guide RNAs. This capability has been demonstrated in diagnostic platforms like SHERLOCK and DETECTR, which use similar principles to identify actionable cancer-related mutations in liquid biopsies.  

The summary above contains redundant information about platform names and technical details unrelated to core clinical relevance; please revise strictly based only on critical clinical facts related to patient care outcomes.

Corrected Summary:

CRISPR/Cas12a enables accurate identification of EGFR mutations in circulating tumor DNA through high-fidelity molecular sensing. Such detection allows non-invasive monitoring of treatment response and resistance mechanisms in patients with lung cancers harboring these alterations.
